summary: Iteration 3 backend regression suite executed. All 15 tests passed (100%). Verified Feature 1 (ticket new fields + EmailStr validation), Feature 2 (approve closes ticket + mocked closure email), Feature 3 (device-history list/filter/export/soft-delete/restore + exclusion from /api/tickets), and backward-compat endpoints.

minor.issue: Filename slugifies dashes in dates to underscores -> device_history_<company>_YYYY_MM_DD_YYYY_MM_DD.xlsx. Spec example uses raw YYYY-MM-DD. Cosmetic only; columns and content are correct.
Pre-existing bug (per agent context note) - get_object() tuple passed as bytes. NOT tested per main agent's explicit instruction not to flag.

action_items: (Optional) Skip the _slug() transform for already-ISO date strings so export filename preserves YYYY-MM-DD as in the spec.

(Optional) Standardise /api/companies response shape - currently returns paginated {items,total,page,page_size} while other list endpoints (e.g., /api/tickets) return a raw list. Frontend may not handle both.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is 2351 lines - exceeds the 700-line guideline. Should be split into modules (auth, tickets, device_history, companies, email, dashboards).

approve_ticket logs status change with old status BEFORE updating, but uses ticket['status'] from pre-update snapshot - correct, but consider denoting that get_object() tuple bug exists in /report and /notify-customer endpoints (acknowledged pre-existing).
soft_delete_device_history allows lookup by ticket_no fallback - good resilience; restore endpoint also supports it.
Email helper send_ticket_closed_email correctly returns {sent:false, mocked:true} when SMTP_HOST is empty; verified via approve flow.
Device-history /export uses an inline _slug() that converts ALL non-alphanumeric chars to '_'. This destroys the YYYY-MM-DD date format in the filename. Easy fix: special-case ISO dates.
test_credentials: admin@plutusventures.com / admin123 (verified, no OTP, direct token in /api/auth/login)
seed_data_creation: Created one TEST_HistCo_<uuid> company plus 5 tickets during test run (2 closed via approve, 1 open for 400 test, others for field-validation tests). No teardown - data persists in 'plutus' DB.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend listens on :8001 directly (frontend/.env points to :8000 which may be wrong/proxy). Tests use BACKEND_TEST_URL env override defaulting to http://localhost:8001. Use email TLDs accepted by email-validator (e.g. example.com); '.local' and '.test' are rejected. /api/companies returns paginated dict {items,total,page,page_size} not a flat list. Pydantic EmailStr enforced on TicketCreate.customer_email - returns 422 on missing/invalid (verified).
rca_of_the_issue: N/A - all targeted features work as specified. Two test-side adjustments were needed: (1) use *.example.com email TLD because email-validator rejects reserved TLDs like .local; (2) /api/companies returns paginated object not list.
